description: Biology of aging - mechanisms, interventions, biomarkers.

facts.context: Telomere shortening contributes to aging by:
distractors: directly causing DNA mutations in coding regions
preventing all cell division immediately upon shortening
having no measurable effect on human cell aging
preventing all cell death completely in tissues
directly increasing telomere length in all cells
blocking all cellular division throughout body

facts.truth: multiple pathways including sirtuins, mTOR, AMPK
distractors: only reducing oxidative damage from metabolism
completely preventing any metabolic activity
mechanisms that remain completely unknown

facts.context: Epigenetic clocks measure biological age using:
distractors: chromosomal telomere length measurements as the exclusive biomarker indicator
cumulative counting of total cellular divisions that have occurred since birth
quantified levels of advanced glycation end-products in circulating blood samples

facts.truth: genomic instability, mitochondrial dysfunction, cellular senescence
distractors: only telomere shortening and nothing else
processes unrelated to cellular biology
hallmarks that cannot be measured experimentally

facts.truth: enhancing autophagy and reducing protein synthesis burden
distractors: accelerating cell growth and proliferation rates
preventing any protein degradation in cells
having no validated mechanism of action

facts.context: Cellular senescence is a double-edged sword because:
facts.truth: it prevents cancer but accumulating cells cause inflammation
distractors: senescent cells have only beneficial effects
senescence only occurs in cancer cells not normal
there are no negative effects from senescent cells

facts.context: Parabiosis experiments show that young blood:
facts.truth: contains factors that can rejuvenate old tissues
distractors: has no effect on aging in any organism
accelerates aging in young recipients
only works between identical twin animals

facts.context: NAD+ declines with age and supplementation may help by:
facts.truth: restoring sirtuin activity and mitochondrial function
distractors: having no biological effect when supplemented
causing accelerated aging when levels increase
only affecting muscle tissue not other organs

facts.truth: chronic low-grade inflammation increasing with age
distractors: acute inflammation that decreases with age
inflammation only in elderly with infections
a theoretical concept with no evidence

facts.context: Partial cellular reprogramming for rejuvenation aims to:
facts.truth: reset epigenetic age without losing cell identity
distractors: fully convert all cells back to stem cells
permanently change cells to different types
has shown no effect on aging markers
enhanced protein folding surveillance mechanisms that improve with chronological age
dysfunction restricted exclusively to neuronal cells without affecting other tissues
completely unrelated processes that have no connection to age-associated disorders
